text: Abstract Abstract PDF This paper discusses the effects of water quality on humans, animals, plants, industries and the environment as well as the contamination and pollution of water resources which have become prevalent in the last few decades. Also in this paper, an attempt is made to determine the Water Quality Index (WQI) and the Water Quality Classification (WQC). In addition, parameters optimization and tuning were used to improve the performances of several machine learning algorithms for determining the Water Quality Index (WQI) and the classification of Water Quality Classification (WQC). Grid search was used to optimize and tune the parameters of the four classification models (RF, Xgboost, GB and AdaBoost) as well as the parameters of the four regression models (KNN regressor, DT regressor, SVR and MLP regressor). The performance of the proposed machine learning algorithms were evaluated based on their accuracy in determining the classification and regression of water quality resources. Also, preprocessing techniques such as data imputation (mean imputation) and data normalization were applied to the data in order to prepare it for the proposed models. In this paper, the used data set had 7 features and 1991 instances. The performance metrics that were used to assess the efficiency of the classification models were the accuracy, recall, precision, MCC (Matthews's Correlation Coefficient) and F1 score. The performance metrics that were used to assess the efficiency of the regression models were the MAE (Mean Absolute Error), MedAE (Median Absolute Error), MSE (Mean Square Error) and R 2 (coefficient of determination). According to the testing results of the models, the GB model provided the best results for the classification of the water quality resources with an accuracy of 99.50% for predicting the values of WQC. Also, the results indicated that the MLP regressor model provided the best results for the regression models for predicting the values of the WQI with an R 2 value of 99.8%.
